mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >60% of instances when FEDFUNDS increases by >0.5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, which reduce corporate earnings growth prospects and lower equity prices, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >60% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic expansion, boosting corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 when both GDPC1 and INDPRO show positive growth over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising FEDFUNDS and elevated CPIAUCSL create stagflationary pressures that depress equity earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise by >0.5% and 0.3% respectively over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates tight labor markets, which drive wage inflation and corporate cost pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive when PAYEMS > 150k and UNRATE < 4% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) reflect strong consumer demand for residential construction, which boosts economic activity and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when HOUST increases by >5% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Surging real sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when RSAFS exceeds 700,000 for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals weak supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces corporate output and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive when INDPRO falls below 100 for 3 consecutive months.
